symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe back pain with inability to weight-bear after trivial bending
symptoms.character: sudden sharp crunching pain superimposed on a deep ache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fracture from minimal force signals underlying bone pathology.
Lytic bone disease is a defining CRAB feature; a fragility fracture is a common presenting event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Subacute deep bone pain in back and ribs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bone pain from lytic lesions is the commonest myeloma symptom.
Progressive multi-site bone pain is not typical of simple mechanical strain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss (~5 kg) with months of fatigue and malaise

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional decline and anaemia-related fatigue are common in myeloma.
Weight loss and malaise are non-specific malignancy markers.
Marrow infiltration causing thrombocytopenia produces easy bruising.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent pneumonia (two episodes in a year)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Immune paresis (hypogammaglobulinaemia) predisposes to recurrent bacterial infection.
Light-chain cast nephropathy causes proteinuria and renal impairment (the R of CRAB).

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved lower-limb sensation and intact bladder/bowel control

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact sensation and sphincter function argue against completed cord compression now — but do not exclude imminent compression from an unstable fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised bony tenderness over L3/L4 spinous processes and left lower ribs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal spinal tenderness localises the fracture — a clinician palpation finding, sealed and not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced lower-limb power 4/5 (pain-limited), symmetrical reflexes, intact sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Power limited by pain rather than true paraparesis; a clinician neurological exam, not available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Conjunctival and general pallor with tachycardia (HR 105)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective pallor and tachycardia point to anaemia; a measured clinician finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Monoclonal paraprotein (M-protein) on serum/urine protein electrophoresis

investigation_details.investigation_name: Serum and urine protein electrophoresis with serum free light chains
investigation_details.expected_result: Monoclonal band / abnormal free light-chain ratio
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Myeloma undiagnosed; the defining biomarker is never obtainable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An M-protein with clonal marrow plasma cells is diagnostic — a hospital/pathology investigation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Punched-out lytic bone lesions and fracture on skeletal imaging

investigation_details.investigation_name: Skeletal survey / whole-body low-dose CT / MRI spine
investigation_details.expected_result: Lytic lesions; vertebral fracture; assessment of cord compression
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Fracture stability and cord compression cannot be assessed; imaging is unavailable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lytic lesions are a CRAB bone criterion; MRI defines cord-compression risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypercalcaemia, anaemia and renal impairment on bloods

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood examination, calcium, renal function
investigation_details.expected_result: Anaemia, raised calcium, impaired renal function (CRAB)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: End-organ damage undetected; bloods are unavailable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The CRAB biochemistry confirms end-organ damage — hospital pathology only.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bone pain, constitutional decline, bruising, recurrent infection and proteinuria together strongly suggest a plasma-cell dyscrasia and should prompt urgent work-up — but the AI recognises the pattern, it does not confirm the diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Unstable pathological fracture with cord-compression risk
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A pathological fracture with currently-intact neurology is the time-critical emergency driver — immobilise and escalate before compression completes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency in-person assessment with MRI whole spine
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines fracture stability and cord-compression status — the immediate emergency question.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum/urine protein electrophoresis and serum free light chains
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The defining myeloma biomarker; arranged through the emergency/haematology pathway, never via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood examination, calcium, renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects CRAB end-organ damage (anaemia, hypercalcaemia, renal impairment).
